macro_segments.title: Opening Source and Background on Hand and Foot Sanctification

micro_segments.title: Parashat Tzav source text (Shemot 30:19-20) and its exegetical purpose
Why the phrase 'lehaktir isheh laHashem' is added: to teach about work that does not require atonement
Dispute between Rabbi and Rabbi Elazar berabbi Shimon on whether overnight invalidates sanctification

macro_segments.title: The Question of Water in the Kiyor (Basin) Remaining Overnight

micro_segments.title: Ilfa's dilemma: does linah (overnight) invalidate water in the kiyor?
First explanation: hands and feet themselves do not become pasul, so water in kiyor may be unaffected
Alternative: kiyor becomes part of service vessels and subject to overnight invalidation
Ravin's report of Rabbi Yochanan via Rav Ami: Ilfa's dilemma parallels the Rabbi/Rabbi Elazar dispute
Rabbi Yitzchak bar Bisna objects that this doesn't resolve the first problem
Rav Assi's teaching: kiyor not lowered overnight is valid for night service but not next day
Gemara rejects this as insufficient proof without clarifying the reason

macro_segments.title: Ben Kattin's Mechanical Innovation: Twelve-Spigot Kiyor and Pulley System

micro_segments.title: Ben Kattin expanded kiyor from two spigots to twelve to accommodate multiple kohanim
Ben Kattin created pulley system to lower kiyor to spring at night, preventing overnight invalidation
Gemara questions: is this practice according to Rabbi or Rabbi Elazar berabbi Shimon?
Gemara concludes: practice reflects Rabbi's view that overnight does invalidate

macro_segments.title: Geography of the Temple: Definition of 'Tzafon' (North) for Korban Service

micro_segments.title: Positioning of the par (bull) on Yom Kippur between ulam and mizbe'ach, facing west
Definition of tzafon (north): from kohen gadol's perspective and competing opinions
Rabbi Yossi bar Rabbi Yehuda's definition vs. Rabbi Elazar bar Rabbi Shimon's extension
Reason for positioning: kohen gadol's weakness on Yom Kippur, not definitional proof

macro_segments.title: Terumat HaDeshen and Next-Day Sanctification Requirements

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan: kohen who sanctified for terumat hadeshen needs not sanctify again next day
Abaye's interpretation: Rabbi Yochanan follows Rabbi's view, with linah not invalidating from kriyas hagever to dawn
Rava's reinterpretation: Rabbi Yochanan follows Rabbi Elazar berabbi Shimon, but only for beginning of service
Chronological issues: how can 3rd-century Amora dispute 2nd-century Tanna?

macro_segments.title: Mishna from Masechet Tamid: Kohanim Rush to Wash When Service Begins

micro_segments.title: Brethren kohanim see kohen removing ashes, run to kiyor to sanctify hands and feet
Ambiguity: does this support Rabbi or Rabbi Elazar berabbi Shimon?
Gemara resolves: these are new kohanim (kohanei chadati) who have never sanctified before
Resolution leaves both views potentially supported; no clear proof emerges

macro_segments.title: The Question of Yetziah (Leaving the Temple) and Re-sanctification

micro_segments.title: Central question: does leaving the temple azarah invalidate prior sanctification?
Two interpretive approaches: separation itself vs. distraction (hasachat ha-da'at)
First ta shema: if hands and feet leave but body remains inside, does sanctification persist?
Gemara rejects first ta shema: question requires full body leaving, not just hands
Second ta shema: incorrect sanctification (outside temple, non-service vessel) makes service pasul
Gemara rejects second ta shema: not addressing leaving after proper sanctification inside
Third ta shema from Tosefta: kohen leaving azarah for long time needs tevilah; brief leave needs re-sanctification
Rav Pappa rejects third ta shema: example involves bathroom; already taught in Yoma
Fourth ta shema: Para Aduma (red heifer) service outside temple; dispute on sanctification requirements
Rav Pappa on Para Aduma: entire service outside; unclear why sanctification needed at all
No resolution reached; question remains open for continued study

topical_tags.term: Kiddush yadayim veraglayim (hand and foot sanctification)
Rabbi vs Rabbi Elazar berabbi Shimon
